heroHeadline: Oil Rebounds to $92–94 After Crash; Goolsbee Says Rate Cuts May Wait Until 2027; FOMC/ECB 10 Days Away
heroSubtitle: Apr 19: WTI crude oil rebounded to ~$92–94/bbl from the Apr 17 crash low ($85.57), as markets questioned whether Hormuz is truly 'fully open' — Brent climbing back toward $98. US Customs launching CAPE tariff refund portal Apr 20-21 for $166B in IEEPA refunds (8.9% effective rate persists under Section 122). Fed's Goolsbee warned stagflation is 'the worst situation for a central bank' and rate cuts may need to slip to 2027. S&P 500 at record 7,126.06 (Apr 17 Friday close). 10 days to the FOMC Apr 29-30 stagflation decision and ECB Apr 30 rate decision — the most consequential synchronized central bank week since 2022. JPMorgan: 'no longer sees a US recession.' Moody's: 49%. Trump-Xi Beijing summit confirmed May 14-15.
footerNote: Data sourced from IMF WEO Apr 2026, BIS, Federal Reserve FOMC, ECB Governing Council, BEA, BLS, World Bank, Tax Foundation, Penn Wharton Budget Model, Conference Board, University of Michigan Surveys of Consumers, CBOE, Goldman Sachs Research, JPMorgan Global Markets Strategy, Moody's Analytics, IEA, EIA, China NBS, Semafor, NPR, CNBC, Atlantic Council
